title: Clouds
description: Optional cloud attenuation layers. Each layer generates an independent procedural cloud density field, binary mask, optical depth field, and transmission field. Multiple enabled layers are combined as an atmospheric stack: transmission multiplies, optical depth adds, masks are OR'd together, and density is combined as layered opacity.

type.description: Cloud layer type. Presets (`patchy`, `cellular`, `veil`, `sheet`, and `fog`) provide tuned defaults for cloud shape and optical depth. Use `custom` to start from generic defaults and directly specify model parameters.

name.description: Optional human-readable metadata label for this layer. The name is used in generated metadata and diagnostics only; it does not affect cloud generation. Defaults to `cloud_<index>`.

enabled.description: Whether to include this layer when generating the combined cloud field. Disabled layers are ignored as if they were not present.

seed.description: Optional deterministic seed for this layer's noise field. If omitted and a simulation seed is present, SatSim derives a stable seed from the simulation seed and layer index. If no simulation seed is present, SatSim generates a random cloud seed for the run and records it in metadata.

coverage.description: Target fractional cloud amount for this layer, from 0.0 for clear to 1.0 for full coverage. This is a fraction only; percent values such as 20 are not accepted.

texture.description: Grouped cloud texture controls. These fields are aliases for the legacy flat texture fields on this same layer. Do not set the same logical field both directly and inside texture.

optical.description: Grouped optical-depth controls. These fields are aliases for the legacy flat tau fields on this same layer. Do not set the same logical field both directly and inside optical.

geometry.description: Grouped cloud-plane geometry controls. These fields are aliases for the legacy flat range and altitude fields on this same layer. Do not set the same logical field both directly and inside geometry.

motion.description: Grouped cloud advection controls. These fields are aliases for the legacy flat wind fields on this same layer. Do not set the same logical field both directly and inside motion.

illumination.description: Grouped cloud brightness controls. These fields are aliases for the legacy flat brightness field on this same layer. Do not set the same logical field both directly and inside illumination.

feature_scales_m.description: Positive physical texture scales, in meters, used by the multi-scale procedural noise model. Smaller values add fine texture; larger values create broader cloud structures.

density_edge_width.description: Nonnegative softness width for mapping procedural texture into cloud density. Lower values create sharper cloud edges; higher values create broader, softer transitions.

density_floor.description: Optional minimum density added before optical-depth mapping. Use null for no explicit floor. Positive values are useful for veil, sheet, or fog-like layers where the frame should retain some baseline attenuation.

brightness.description: Optional cloud glow brightness in visual magnitude per arcsec^2, used to simulate artificial ground light illuminating the cloud. Lower magnitude values are brighter. Emitted photoelectrons are scaled by the inverted layer transmission, `1 - transmission`.

range.description: Optional cloud slant range in kilometers. This controls the physical size of cloud texture projected onto the FPA and converts wind speed into pixels per second. Defaults to 3.0.

altitude.description: Optional cloud height in kilometers above the observer, used by source-driven cloud brightening gains. If omitted, SatSim uses the layer range as the source-height proxy.

wind_speed.description: Optional cloud advection speed in meters per second on the cloud plane. The value is converted to pixels per second from the cloud range and FPA IFOV. Defaults to 0.0, meaning no wind-driven cloud motion.

wind_direction.description: Optional cloud advection direction in degrees using SatSim's image-plane polar convention: 0 moves cloud features toward positive column/right, 90 moves them toward positive row/down. Values are normalized modulo 360 degrees. Defaults to 0.0.

texture_contrast.description: Contrast applied to the normalized procedural texture before density mapping. A value of 1.0 preserves full texture variation; lower values flatten mottling and create smoother haze-like layers.

locality_degree.description: Positive integer controlling cloud support localization by multiplying independent texture fields. A value of 1 keeps broad coherent structure; higher values make cloud support more localized and patchy.

tau_min.description: Minimum optical depth used when converting nonzero cloud density into Beer-Lambert transmission. Higher values make even faint cloud regions more attenuating.

tau_max.description: Maximum optical depth reached by dense cloud regions. Higher values produce stronger attenuation and can make thick cloud regions nearly opaque. Runtime validation requires tau_max >= tau_min.

tau_gamma.description: Positive response exponent for mapping density into optical depth. Values below 1.0 make lower-density cloud regions more optically significant; values above 1.0 concentrate attenuation in denser regions.

feature_scales_m.description: Positive physical texture scales, in meters. Alias of clouds[].feature_scales_m.

scales_m.description: Positive physical texture scales, in meters. Alias of clouds[].feature_scales_m.

density_edge_width.description: Nonnegative softness width for mapping procedural texture into cloud density. Alias of clouds[].density_edge_width.

edge_width.description: Nonnegative softness width for mapping procedural texture into cloud density. Alias of clouds[].density_edge_width.

density_floor.description: Optional minimum density added before optical-depth mapping. Alias of clouds[].density_floor.

floor.description: Optional minimum density added before optical-depth mapping. Alias of clouds[].density_floor.

texture_contrast.description: Contrast applied to the normalized procedural texture. Alias of clouds[].texture_contrast.

contrast.description: Contrast applied to the normalized procedural texture. Alias of clouds[].texture_contrast.

locality_degree.description: Positive integer controlling cloud support localization. Alias of clouds[].locality_degree.

tau_min.description: Minimum optical depth used when converting nonzero cloud density into Beer-Lambert transmission. Alias of clouds[].tau_min.

tau_max.description: Maximum optical depth reached by dense cloud regions. Alias of clouds[].tau_max.

tau_gamma.description: Positive response exponent for mapping density into optical depth. Alias of clouds[].tau_gamma.

range.description: Cloud slant range in kilometers. Alias of clouds[].range.

range_km.description: Cloud slant range in kilometers. Alias of clouds[].range.

altitude.description: Cloud height in kilometers above the observer. Alias of clouds[].altitude.

altitude_km.description: Cloud height in kilometers above the observer. Alias of clouds[].altitude.

wind_speed.description: Cloud advection speed in meters per second on the cloud plane. Alias of clouds[].wind_speed.

speed_m_per_s.description: Cloud advection speed in meters per second on the cloud plane. Alias of clouds[].wind_speed.

wind_direction.description: Cloud advection direction in degrees using SatSim's image-plane polar convention. Alias of clouds[].wind_direction.

direction_deg.description: Cloud advection direction in degrees using SatSim's image-plane polar convention. Alias of clouds[].wind_direction.

brightness.description: Cloud glow brightness in visual magnitude per arcsec^2. Alias of clouds[].brightness.

brightness_mag_arcsec2.description: Cloud glow brightness in visual magnitude per arcsec^2. Alias of clouds[].brightness.
